Intern, AI Deployment & Microsoft Copilo.Job Description::
Intern, AI Deployment & Microsoft Copilot Enablement Co-op

POSITION SUMMARY

Working under the supervision of the Director, Operational Excellence, and receiving day-to-day guidance from the Operational Excellence team and Mitacs AI Strategy Team members, this role will support Mitacs' organization-wide AI deployment by helping business teams adopt approved Microsoft Copilot tools in practical, responsible, and measurable ways.

The co-op will assist with identifying, documenting, testing, and scaling AI use cases across business teams, with a primary focus on and Microsoft 365 Copilot capabilities available through Mitacs-approved tools. The role will support business teams in translating real work problems into practical Copilot use cases, developing simple training and enablement materials, capturing lessons learned, and helping staff build confidence using AI safely in day-to-day work.

This role offers a unique opportunity to contribute to Mitacs' AI adoption journey by supporting practical use-case development, training, change enablement, and knowledge sharing across departments.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Support Mitacs' AI deployment by helping business teams identify practical opportunities to use approved Microsoft Copilot tools in everyday work.
Work with department Change Agents, Change Champions, and business teams to document AI use cases connected to strategic and corporate plan priorities.
Help translate business needs into clear Copilot use-case descriptions, including the problem to solve, how Copilot may help, expected benefits, responsible-use considerations, and next steps.
Assist teams in using Microsoft M365 Copilot Chat and Microsoft 365 Copilot capabilities for common workplace scenarios such as drafting, summarizing, brainstorming, meeting preparation, presentation development, document review, data interpretation, and internal communications.
Develop practical examples, prompt guides, quick-reference materials, and before-and-after use-case stories that help staff understand how to use Copilot effectively.
Support training sessions, workshops, office hours, and demos for business teams on approved Copilot tools, prompting basics, responsible AI use, and practical use-case development.
Help gather, organize, and maintain AI enablement materials on the Mitacs AI Strategy SharePoint site, including training links, approved-use guidance, FAQs, use-case examples, and Change Agent resources.
Support the development of use-case libraries and reusable templates that help teams start small, test safely, learn from experiments, and scale what works.
Assist with documenting AI adoption learnings, common questions, barriers, user feedback, and recommended improvements to training and enablement materials.
Support tracking and reporting of AI deployment activities, including training completion, use-case progress, adoption indicators, and examples of business value.
Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders, including Operational Excellence, Technology, Change Agents, Change Champions, and business teams, to support responsible and practical AI adoption.
Present summaries, findings, training materials, and recommendations to the Operational Excellence team, Mitacs AI Strategy Team members, and project stakeholders.
Document methodologies, use-case examples, training materials, and recommendations to support knowledge transfer and sustainable AI adoption.

Expected Deliverables:

Microsoft Copilot use-case summaries for business teams, including problem statements, Copilot-enabled approaches, expected benefits, responsible-use considerations, and next steps.
Practical prompt guides and quick-reference materials for common Mitacs business scenarios.
Training and enablement materials for Microsoft M365 Copilot Chat and Microsoft 365 Copilot use in everyday work.
Workshop, demo, or office-hours support materials for business teams and Change Agents.
AI use-case library content, including examples of how teams can apply Copilot to drafting, summarizing, meeting preparation, presentation development, document review, data interpretation, and communications.
Updated AI Strategy SharePoint resources, FAQs, training links, templates, and enablement content.
Documentation of lessons learned, common user questions, adoption barriers, and recommendations for improving AI enablement.
Summaries of AI deployment activity, use-case progress, and practical recommendations for scaling adoption across Mitacs.
Intern, AI Deployment & Microsoft Copilo.Qualifications::
ROLE REQUIREMENTS

Currently enrolled in Business, Management, Information Systems, Data Analytics, Digital Transformation, Communications, Education/Training, Engineering, or a related program.
Demonstrable interest or experience using Generative AI tools, especially Microsoft Copilot or comparable tools, to analyze information, draft content, solve problems, or improve productivity.
Intermediate to advanced understanding of AI concepts, including generative AI, large language models, prompting, responsible AI use, and practical workplace applications.
Strong interest in helping non-technical business users adopt AI tools confidently, safely, and effectively.
Ability to translate business problems into practical AI use cases, simple workflows, examples, and training materials.
Strong communication, facilitation, presentation, and documentation skills.
Ability to create clear user guides, job aids, templates, FAQs, and training resources for business audiences.
Proficiency with Microsoft 365 tools such as Word, PowerPoint, Excel, Teams, SharePoint, Outlook, and Copilot-enabled workflows.
Experience developing training materials, running workshops, supporting change management, or helping users adopt new digital tools considered an asset.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to organize feedback, identify patterns, and recommend practical improvements.
Commitment to privacy, confidentiality, responsible AI use, and human review of AI-generated outputs.
Ability to work independently while collaborating effectively within a cross-functional team.

Intern, Industrial Engineering & AI Co-o.Job Description::
Intern, Industrial Engineering & AI Co-op

POSITION SUMMARY

Working under the supervision of the Director, Operational Excellence, and receiving day-to-day guidance from the Operational Excellence Advisor leading the Operational Productivity Visibility initiative, this role will support the analysis and improvement of operational processes through data analytics, Industrial Engineering principles, and Artificial Intelligence (AI).

The co-op will assist in estimating cycle times, analyzing process variations, developing operational analytics, and evaluating AI-assisted approaches to extract meaningful insights from operational data and screen recordings. The role offers a unique opportunity to contribute to an innovative initiative focused on building sustainable operational visibility and supporting continuous improvement across Service Delivery.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Support cycle time estimation and operational analysis for business processes.
Analyze operational workflows to identify process variations, bottlenecks, and improvement opportunities.
Conduct time studies, activity sampling, and process observation to estimate task-level cycle time, touch time, wait time, rework time, and handoff delays
Apply basic statistical analysis to understand averages, medians, variation, outliers, trends, and drivers of cycle time performance.
Analyze operational workflows to identify bottlenecks, rework loops, process variation, workload patterns, and improvement opportunities.
Assist in developing dashboards, reports, and visualizations using tools such as Excel and Power BI.
Organize, clean, and analyze operational datasets to support project reporting and decision-making.
Explore and evaluate AI tools and techniques to automate analysis and generate operational insights from structured data and screen recordings.
Assist in developing standardized task libraries, analytical methodologies, and supporting documentation.
Present findings and recommendations to the Operational Excellence team and project stakeholders.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support process improvement initiatives and pilot activities.
Document analyses, methodologies, and recommendations to ensure knowledge transfer and sustainability.

Expected Deliverables:

Current-state process maps and/or value stream maps.
Cycle time, touch time, wait time, and handoff delay estimates.
Analysis of process variation, bottlenecks, rework loops, and workload patterns.
Excel or Power BI dashboards to support operational productivity visibility.
Standardized task library and process measurement definitions.
Documented methodology for cycle time estimation and ongoing process measurement.
AI-assisted analysis approach for structured data, process documentation, and screen recordings.
Summary of findings and practical recommendations for process improvement.
Intern, Industrial Engineering & AI Co-o.Qualifications::
ROLE REQUIREMENTS

Currently enrolled in Industrial Engineering, Systems Engineering, Management Engineering, Operations Management, Business Analytics, Data Analytics, or a related program.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and experience working with data.
Experience with Power BI or other data visualization tools considered an asset.
Demonstrable interest or experience using Generative AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Claude, Gemini) to analyze information, solve problems, or automate tasks.
Basic understanding of AI concepts, including Large Language Models (LLMs), prompt engineering, and practical AI applications.
Familiarity with Lean, Continuous Improvement, Process Improvement, or Industrial Engineering principles.
Excellent communication, presentation, and organizational skills.
Ability to work independently while collaborating effectively within a cross-functional team.

AI and Automation Co-op Student, Transfo.Job Description::
Reporting to the Director, Enterprise Transformation, the AI and Automation Co-op Student will support the Transformation Management Office (TMO) by identifying, testing, continuing, scaling, and delivery AI-enabled solutions that enhance transformation operations and program delivery.

This role focuses on operationalizing AI within real workflows, ensuring continuity of existing work while developing new high-value use cases that improve efficiency, insight generation, and decision-making within the TMO and the Mitacs Enterprise Transformation. The successful candidate will evaluate emerging AI capabilities and translate them into practical tools, workflows, and use cases that improve efficiency, insight generation, and operational effectiveness within the Transformation Management Office.

Solutions developed in this role are expected to support real operational workflows.

The position provides exposure to enterprise transformation governance while offering the opportunity to prototype and operationalize AI-enabled improvements within the TMO environment.

Why This Role?

This co-op role offers the opportunity to:

Continue and scale AI solutions currently in development
Apply artificial intelligence tools to real operational and program management challenges
Contribute to organization-wide transformation initiatives
Work directly with transformation leadership and gain exposure to program governance and enterprise change delivery
Prototype, test, and implement AI enabled solutions that improve Transformation Management Office operational workflows
Explore how emerging AI capabilities can be applied to complex operational and organizational challenges

Key Responsibilities

Applied AI and Workflow Innovation

Evaluate emerging AI tools and capabilities that can support transformation operations and program management
Prototype, test, and iterate on AI-enabled workflows that improve operational efficiency and reduce manual effort
Map opportunities to apply AI and design solutions to support reporting, documentation, scheduling, stakeholder communication, and operational processes
Translate experimentation into practical AI use cases that can be implemented within Mitacs
Document findings and provide recommendations for scalable adoption

Transformation Program Operations

Support Transformation Management Office activities related to enterprise transformation initiatives
Contribute to improving program tracking, reporting, and information management processes
Develop AI-assisted approaches to meeting summaries, action tracking, and documentation
Support improvements to knowledge management and operational workflows

Data & Reporting

Assist in organizing and structuring program data to support reporting and analysis
Explore AI-enabled approaches to synthesizing program updates, risks, issues, and key insights
Contribute to the development of improved reporting approaches that leverage AI capabilities

Screening Question to add to the cover letter

"Describe a project, experiment, or tool you built using AI. What problem were you trying to solve and what approach did you take?"

Marketing Statement

Join a company that has been a part of moving Metro Vancouver for over 35 years.

British Columbia Rapid Transit Company Ltd. (BCRTC) maintains and operates the Expo and Millennium SkyTrain Lines from our central facilities in south Burnaby. Our services connect downtown Vancouver to the cities of Burnaby, New Westminster, Coquitlam, Port Moody and Surrey. With the future expansion of both the Expo and Millennium Lines gathering pace, infrastructure renewal and new asset acquisition projects underway, this is an exciting time to join our team of over 1,200 dedicated employees, working on one of the longest fully-automated rapid transit systems in the world.

Our work encompasses roles such as administration, engineering, elevator and escalator maintenance, field operations, vehicle maintenance, and wayside (station and guideway) maintenance.

When you work for SkyTrain, not only will you help move Metro Vancouver, you will also have access to comprehensive benefits including paid vacation, medical benefits, transit passes, training and more. We are a proud equal opportunity employer, committed to a diverse and inclusive workforce.

Come explore what makes us one of BC's Top Employers!

Responsibilities

This four (4) month assignment will provide opportunities for engagement with engineers, managers and others in different departments within the organization, and provide an understanding of some of the workings within the engineering department at BCRTC.

This position will provide assistance to Engineering with various projects related to maintaining and operating BCRTC rolling stock and rail assets. This includes research of mechanical or electrical issues on rolling stock and wayside elements, research and development of design standards and process improvements and compliance.

Intuit is the global financial technology platform that powers prosperity for the people and communities we serve. At Intuit, we believe in the power of early talent. Our internship and co-op program provides hands-on experience, mentorship, and opportunities to contribute to real-world projects that impact millions. We get up every morning with our hearts beating fast, driven by the knowledge that we're transforming the world of consumer and small business finance. It's why our products continue to resonate with 50 million consumers around the globe. We are a Fortune 500 fintech company, and Canada's Best Places to work several years in a row.

Our TurboTax MarTech and Data Activation team is at the heart of our marketing efforts. We're responsible for enabling and managing tracking for in-market campaign activities, data onboarding and activation, audience segmentation, and implementing new marketing technologies. Our goal is to empower our go-to-market teams with the capabilities and tools they need to execute impactful campaigns.




Intuit values fresh perspectives and innovative ideas that early talent brings. Join us and contribute to projects that shape the future of financial technology!

Platform Migration Support: Play a key role in supporting platform migration, collaborating with key stakeholders across multiple craft skills.
Data Quality Assurance: Partner with business stakeholders to perform data assurance and data quality checks and help resolve issues if/when discrepancies arise.
Process development and evolution: work with stakeholders to help evolve existing processes to account for new technologies.
Collaborative Impact: Work closely with cross-functional teams to understand their data needs and support the successful execution of marketing campaigns.

Software Engineering or Computer Science.Job Title:: Software Engineering or Computer Science Co-op Student
Software Engineering or Computer Science.Job Description:: The Software Developer co-op position at DSA Ocean will support its Software team in developing and testing software products. The position will see the student contribute to new software products currently in development, including software development, debugging, and testing.
Software Engineering or Computer Science.Qualifications::
Be enrolled in a Computer Science or Software Engineering program at an accredited institution in Canada
Have training or experience in developing software using languages such as C#, Java, or C++
Have an interest in developing reliable software and technology
Experience with Microsoft Visual Studio
A willingness to learn and ask questions
Have an interest in hands-on software development and problem solving - using AI to support your work, while developing your own development skills
A combination of technical aptitude and practical thinking
An attention to detail and ability to work independently
Be able represent both themselves and DSA Ocean in a professional manner
Have a love for the ocean, and an understanding of the marine environment



Company Profile
DSA Ocean is a Canadian ocean engineering consultancy and software technology company with its head office in Victoria, BC. Since 2006, our clients have trusted DSA Ocean with their routine to their most challenging ocean engineering problems. DSA Ocean provides practical engineering services that enable ocean engineers, naval architects, oceanographers - or anybody with business in the water - to assess the effect of currents, winds and waves on their vessels, technologies, and projects. DSA specializes in marine hydrodynamics and mooring design for a range of applications, including oceanography, marine energy, and aquaculture.


DSA Ocean develops and licenses the marine dynamic analysis software ProteusDS for ocean industries.

Key applications include floating tidal energy platforms, fish farms, wave energy converters, and oceanographic moorings.


Position, start date, location
DSA Ocean is seeking a Software Engineering or Computer Science Co-op Student to join its team as a Software Developer, starting early September. This position is for a four-month term, but the ideal candidate would be open to considering an extension to an eight-month term if the opportunity arose. The successful candidate will report to DSA Ocean's Victoria office full-time.


Experience considerations
No previous experience is necessary beyond being enrolled in a Software Engineering or Computer Science degree and having completed at least one full year of undergraduate studies.


Training
On-the-job training will be required due to many of the specialized skills required to fulfill the demands of the position. Thus, candidates not possessing experience are encouraged to apply.


Travel
Travel is not likely required.

Game Developer.Job Description::
About All Out

All Out is building a multiplayer gaming platform where players can discover, play, and share fast, social games, and where developers can build and publish games much faster than traditional game development workflows.

We are building creation tools for a new generation of game makers: a PC editor, AI-native Cloud Build, a custom scripting language built for multiplayer games, world-building tools, a large asset catalog, and one-click multiplayer across iOS, Android, and PC.

Our goal is to make it dramatically faster to go from idea to playable multiplayer game.

About the Role

We're looking for a game developer who can use the All Out editor, our scripting language, and AI tools to quickly turn ideas into playable games.

This role is for someone who loves building. You should be able to take a rough concept, prototype the core loop, test it with players, learn what is fun, and iterate quickly.

The ideal person has a mix of:

game designer brain + technical builder skills + player empathy + speed.

What You'll Do

You will:

Build playable game prototypes inside the All Out editor.
Use All Out's scripting language to create mechanics, player abilities, win conditions, UI, progression, rewards, and multiplayer interactions.
Use AI tools to speed up scripting, debugging, iteration, and content creation.
Turn rough game ideas into playable prototypes in days, not months.
Test prototypes with real players and improve them based on feedback and behavior.
Build games that are simple to understand, fun quickly, and replayable.
Create reusable templates, systems, and example scripts for future games.
Help the product and engineering teams improve the editor, scripting language, AI workflows, and developer experience.
Identify what makes a game work on All Out: retention, clarity, session length, social play, replayability, and monetization.
Work with designers, engineers, artists, and community/developer teams to get prototypes ready for player testing.
Help define best practices for building fast inside All Out.
Create reusable templates, systems, and examples for future games.
Playtest prototypes and quickly fix bugs or confusing mechanics.
Game Developer.Qualifications::
What We're Looking For

You might be a great fit if you:

Love making games and have built prototypes, mods, maps, Roblox experiences, UEFN projects, Unity games, Godot projects, GameMaker projects, or similar.
Are comfortable with scripting or lightweight programming.
Can make something playable quickly without needing perfect specs.
Understand what makes a game fun in the first 30 seconds.
Think deeply about players: what they understand, what they repeat, what they share, and what keeps them coming back.
Care about game feel, pacing, clarity, rewards, stakes, and replayability.
Use AI tools regularly and know how to get useful results from them.
Can debug, iterate, and problem-solve independently.
Are excited to help shape tools that other developers will eventually use.
Like building many prototypes and learning quickly, not spending six months polishing one idea.
Nice to Have

Not required, but helpful:

Experience with Roblox or UEFN
Experience with AI coding tools like Codex, Claude, Cursor
Experience designing progression, upgrades, cosmetics, leaderboards, quests, or simple game economies.
Ability to make simple placeholder UI, icons, thumbnails, or level layouts.
Understanding of UGC platforms and developer ecosystems.
Interest in building tools, templates, and examples for other developers.

About the role

The candidate will participate as a Systems Engineering (SE) member of the System Architecture team in a multidiscipline team environment. The System Architecture team contributes to the total engineering lifecycle from specification, design development, integration and test, deployment, maintenance and field trouble shooting for large scale, high complexity naval combat and platform systems.

The candidate will participate in engineering activities including, but not limited to:

Collaborating on system design activities with senior systems engineering and other engineering staff, including supporting technical reviews
Performing requirements analysis and traceability via the system model
Reviewing requirements, requirements allocation and trade studies
Performing system architecture modelling and design, including Model Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)
Developing automation for MBSE processes
Managing and implementing changes to the system design
Design documentation
Following systems engineering processes and best practices.

About us

Headquartered in Ottawa, Lockheed Martin Canada is the Canadian unit of Lockheed Martin Corporation, a global defense technology company driving innovation and advancing scientific discovery. Our all-domain mission solutions and 21st Century Security® vision accelerate the delivery of transformative technologies to ensure those we serve always stay ahead of ready. We operate major facilities in Ottawa, Montreal, Halifax, Calgary and Victoria and work on a wide range of programs from advanced naval technology products, aircraft sustainment, and unmanned systems software. This position is part of the Rotary and Mission Systems business area, where employees across Canada support engineering, systems integration, training, sustainment and in-service support programs for Canadian and international military customers across land, air and sea.

Pursuing a degree in System Engineering, Software Engineering, Computer Engineering, Computer Science, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering or related field
Candidate must be available for four (4) months, full-time as of September 2026

Communicate effectively with technical and non-technical members of the engineering/management staff and personnel assigned to related work
Aptitude for solving challenging problems in a team environment
Have a high degree of initiative and autonomy
Have a high learning ability

Experience with Agile methods

Intern - AI Software Engineering.Job Description::
Job Description

ABS is one of the largest classification societies in the world. We have more surveyors and engineers than any other class society, and a long history of MUN engineering graduates making their careers with us. The world headquarters for ABS is in Houston, Texas, with major offices in London, Singapore, Athens, Shanghai, and Ottawa. ABS is the in-service classification society for the Canadian Coast Guard (including icebreakers) and the Canadian Navy's Non-combatant vessels, such as the Arctic Offshore Patrol Ships.


The ABS Harsh Environment Technology and Digital Research Centre (HET&DRC) is a special branch of ABS Canda Inc. based in St. John's, NL. The HET&DRC undertakes Research and Development and offers Advisory Services to ship owners around the world with a focus on ships operating in icy waters and harsh atmospheric conditions.


HET&DRC is responsible for providing technical support to the global network of ABS offices and ship owners/operators. Each day brings a multitude of inquiries from diverse clients, ensuring that the work remains consistently engaging and intellectually demanding, attracting bright minds eager to tackle challenges head-on.


HET&DRC continually innovates, creates solutions, issues guidelines, and propels technology development to bolster safety in Polar shipping, preventing environmental pollution and enhancing operational efficiencies. Leveraging cutting-edge advancements and proprietary software, HET&DRC promptly, accurately, and effectively addresses client inquiries, staying true to its mission of serving the greater public interest and meeting the needs of its members and clients while promoting the security of life, property, and preserving the natural environment.


ABS HET&DRC warmly extends an invitation to MUN Computer Science or Engineering students with a strong interest in AI-based software development to apply for a Fall 2026 semester work-term opportunity supporting research on an intelligent ice-monitoring system for ships and evolving the advisory service capabilities. You will have the opportunity to collaborate in identifying and solving new and interesting challenges, thereby enriching your industry knowledge and making a lasting impact.


Location
ABS HET&DRC, St. John's, NL


What you will do
1. Conduct literature review on AI and Machine Learning (ML) based ice-loads prediction methods.
2. Assist in the design and development of an AI-based solver for ice-loads calculation.
3. Assist in the processing, visualization, and interpretation of engineering datasets.
4. Write optimized and efficient code in Python/C++.
5. Assist in the testing, validation, and benchmarking of AI models.
6. Maintain organized project files, including datasets, code, and documentation.
7. Participate in regular project meetings.
8. Collaborate with the ABS HET&DRC engineering team and ABS engineers around the world.
9. Work independently under the guidance of the project supervisor.

Learning Opportunities
1. Exposure to real-world applications of AI and Machine Learning in the marine engineering industry.
2. Hands-on experience in the development of an AI-based ice load solver.
3. Knowledge and experience in training, validation, and deployment of AI models.
4. Knowledge and experience in visualization and statistical analysis of engineering data.
5. Use of advanced programming concepts to optimize code for engineering applications.
6. Experience in collaborating with a multidisciplinary engineering team.
7. Exposure to the professional work environment within a globally recognized company.
Intern - AI Software Engineering.Qualifications::
What You Will Need:

Education and Experience

1. A Graduate (Master's) or undergraduate student with a background in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a similar field.
2. Substantial experience in developing and implementing code.
3. Proficiency in Python is required. Experience with C++ is preferred.
4. Experience with ML frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit-learn) is preferred.
5. Self-driven and able to work independently while collaborating with the HET&DRC team.


Reporting Relationships:

The Internship Program is housed in an HR Cost Center. This position does not have any direct reports.

It is intended to be an accurate reflection of the principal job elements essential for making a fair decision regarding the pay structure of the job.

Software Developer Co-op.Job Description::
Who We Are; What We Do; Where We're Going
Magnet Forensics is a global leader in the development of digital investigative software that acquires, analyzes, and shares evidence from computers, smartphones, tablets, and IoT-related devices. We are continually innovating so our customers can deploy advanced and effective tools to protect their companies, communities, and countries.
Serving thousands of customers globally, our solutions are playing a crucial role in modernizing digital investigations, helping investigators fight crime, protect assets, and guard national security.
With employees based around the world, Magnet Forensics has been expanding our global presence. As a part of Magnet Forensics, you can expect to make a difference in the world, no matter what role you play. You'll be supported through learning and development, not to mention an incredible team with unbelievable talent and integrity.
If you think you would be the right person to join our team working towards this goal, we would love to hear from you!


Role Overview
At Magnet Forensics, you will be fully integrated into our software development team. You'll start by getting comfortable with the code, working through bugs and basic features, all the while writing code that will actually be seen in production. As you ramp, you'll be given more complex features just like any other member of the team. In fact, some of the key features of our software were written by our students!
Software Developer Co-op.Qualifications::
What We Are Looking For
You are a student working towards a degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Software Engineering or a related discipline
You're strong at object-oriented programming (Languages: Java/JavaScript, C#, C++, Python)
You are accountable and take ownership of your projects
You like working in a collaborative environment with smart, funny and interesting people
We hire passionate people who enjoy technical challenges. We love problem solvers who want to keep learning, individually and as part of the team. We want people who collaborate and help others. We foster a team environment where everyone is comfortable sharing their ideas, because we understand that with diversity of thought, we will build the best products.
Compensation & Benefits
At Magnet Forensics, we are committed to fair and consistent pay practices for our student roles. Hourly pay is?determined using standardized, non-negotiable salary bands based on academic progression and the number of official completed work terms recognized by your academic institution.
$22.01 - $33.81 CAD per hour
Salary Range (min - max)
Position Type: Current Vacancy


Indicators of Success
We're looking for someone who checks off most, but not all, of the boxes listed in "skills and experiences". It's more important to us to find candidates who can display indicators of success through skills they have developed and experiences they have been a part of, than to find folks who have "been there, done that". We want to be part of your development journey, and we'll learn as much from you as you learn from us.
How We Work
At Magnet Forensics, we take a hybrid-flexible approach to support your productivity and work-life balance. If you're within a comfortable travel distance to one of our offices, you'll occasionally join us in person. How often you'll come in depends on your department and team needs, typically ranging from weekly to monthly. These in-person moments help us build stronger connections, spark new ideas, and celebrate our successes together. Most days, you can choose what works best for you, while staying in tune with your team's goals.
We're excited to welcome you to our team and look forward to achieving great things together - both in the office and wherever you work best!
The Most Important Thing
We're looking for candidates that can provide examples of how they have demonstrated Magnet CODE in their previous experiences:
CARE - We care about each other and our mission to make a difference in the world.
OWN - We are accountable for our results - while never forgetting to act with integrity, empathy, and respect.
DEDICATE - We put our heart and soul into meeting the needs of our customers and helping them serve the people they protect.
EVOLVE - We are constantly innovating and exploring new ways to work together to make an impact with our work.

This is a student position that is 4 months in duration for the term(s): September-December. You must be a student registered in a Canadian College or University.

Analyst, Finance Insights and Technology.Job Description::
Come make your difference in communities across Canada, where authenticity, trust and making connections is valued - as we shape the future of Canadian retail, together. Our unique position as one of the country's largest employers, coupled with our commitment to positively impact the lives of all Canadians, provides our colleagues a range of opportunities and experiences to help Canadians Live Life Well®.

At Loblaw Companies Limited, we succeed through collaboration and commitment and set a high bar for ourselves and those around us. Whether you are just starting your career, re-entering the workforce, or looking for a new job, this is where you belong.

The pay range for the co-op student role will reflect factors such as the candidate's experience, academic level, and the specific skillset required for the role.

Loblaw has adopted the following hybrid working model (a combination of working between the office and remotely). Colleagues can work remotely up to 1 day a week, while spending the other 4 days in the office (to be determined by each business unit).

The Finance Insights and Technology Engagement (FITE) team at Loblaw is responsible for managing centralized financial reporting for Canada's largest grocery retailer. Our multifaceted team synergize their skills across various key domains, including business intelligence, reporting, analytics, process automation and data governance processes. Reports managed by our team are accessed on multiple platforms including web and mobile and have a vast audience ranging from analysts to C-level executives.

In this role you will be working closely with various groups including IT, Merch, Ops, Costing, Inventory, and our FSPD (Financial Systems and Process Development) team to fulfill the requests of our finance business partners.

We are based in Brampton, Ontario and operate in a closely knit and highly collaborative environment. We are an entrepreneurial, fast-paced, and fun team working to transform the way Loblaw consumes data. To achieve this goal, we are looking for individuals who want to solve challenging problems through data and be able to communicate large amount of complex information in an easy to understand, yet simple to comprehend manner.? We are looking for a talented and motivated individual to join our FITE team. You will be working with various stakeholders to understand their operations, data structures, and reporting requirements. More so, you will be partnering with them to generate and enhance existing reporting. The ability to communicate and present to various levels within the business and to external partners is of utmost importance.?


Responsibilities:

Summarize vast amounts of data to develop reports and models to solve business problems.
Evaluate new data sources and help define more effective ways to communicate data with our business users.
Working with our IT teams to create data processes that are accurate, consistent, and efficient to process large amount of data quickly to meet reporting needs.
Write and optimize SQL queries in Google BigQuery to extract and analyze data for business reporting.
Write SQL to efficiently retrieve information from business data sources to support different kinds of reporting needs.
Follow industry best practices for technology development.
Conduct code and data design reviews within the team, Loblaw IT team and stakeholders.
Design BI technical components based on business and user requirements.
Stakeholder management: Engage with key stakeholders to ensure their reporting needs are fulfilled.

Job Qualifications:

Strong proficiency in SQL for data querying and manipulation to support different kinds of reporting needs.
Hands-on experience with any GCP data services (especially BigQuery, Cloud Storage, or Looker Studio) will be an asset
Strong Microsoft office suite knowledge, specifically in Excel with Pivot table experience, Macro building required, VBA experience would be a benefit.
Having experience using BI, analytical, and database tools would be a benefit. i.e., MicroStrategy, Qlik, Tableau etc.
Ability to Use / Write Python/R/SAS to manage large datasets, perform ad hoc analytics, and build predictive models will be an asset.
Ability to self-organize multiple tasks well and to complete them on time with a clear focus on quantitative results.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Willingness to work well in a fast-paced team environment.
Ability to "think outside the box".

We offer our colleagues progressive careers, comprehensive training, flexibility, and other competitive benefits - these are some of the many reasons why we are one of Canada's Top Employers, Canada's Best Diversity Employers, Canada's Greenest Employers & Canada's Top Employers for Young People.

Reach Technologies has immediate openings for several embedded engineering students to continue development of our Anti-Submarine Warfare (ASW) Software-Defined Radio (SDR) and data acquisition systems, as well as situational awareness systems based on machine learning (ML) in concert with our established team of engineers. We're seeking engineers with strong track records of quickly picking up new skills and technologies in real-time. Experience in systems-level design and implementation

Analog/digital modulation: FM, FSK, PSK, QAM

YOLO, OpenCV, hardware acceleration

Ability to read hardware schematics and HDL
Familiarity with RF lab equipment (e.g., spectrum analyzers, VNAs)
Strong technical writing skills

Why join us?

A proud member of the local Vancouver Island tech community, Reach Technologies Inc. is an established player in the aerospace and defense markets. Our success is based on quality products and exceptional customer service. This success stems from our highly achieving employees, which is why we are devoted to empowering ourselves through professional growth.

At Reach Technologies, employees can expect:

Challenging, exciting, and cutting-edge work
Continuous learning of the latest technologies and their real-world applications

Independence, responsibility, and ownership of project deliverables
Transparent, open management and a true employee-employer partnership
Remote work
Opportunities for personal and professional growth

Extended Medical, flex benefits

Research Internships in Chile.Job Description::
Organization Overview

The Catholic University of Maule (UCM) is an institution that integrates the Council of Rectors and the G9 Network - Non-State Public Universities -, accredited at an advanced level by the National Accreditation Commission in the mandatory areas of Institutional Management and Undergraduate Teaching , and in the elective areas of Research and Outreach.

The close relationship between the lines of research developed by the UCM - from basic and applied science - and the needs present in the territory stands out , which motivates and gives meaning to the scientific work generated in the areas that cover a wide range of disciplines grouped into education, medicine, health sciences, religion and philosophy, engineering, agricultural and forestry sciences, basic sciences and social and economic sciences.

The priority research areas defined by the UCM are education, energy, natural resources, environment, local and human development, health and quality of life , to which it adds as emerging areas of scientific work: artificial intelligence, climate change, science and faith, neurosciences and natural disasters.

MITACS funding - Globalink Research Award: Open to undergraduate and graduate students, the $6000 award allows talented researchers to undertake 12 to 24 week projects with a host supervisor in Canada.

Research Assistant Positions

Universidad Católica del Maule (UCM), offers several options for students from abroad who want to gain research experience at the UCM on a short-term basis. These programs are open to students from all disciplines who are interested in expanding their knowledge in their area of study through collaboration with UCM's researchers.

UCM provides full support for participants before, during and after their placement as well as opportunities to participate in cultural activities to offer a full international exchange. Although every internship is unique, we try to ensure that they provide the following common elements:

Academic activities for two weeks to one year.
A defined project, which creates real value for the host organisation and a valuable learning experience for the student.
Interaction with an assigned supervisor or mentor within the host organisation.

Positions

A full list of internship descriptions can be found here. Please be sure to double check the language(s) each internship is in, as well as the intern requirements. In your cover letter, be sure to clearly state which project you're interested in.

Some examples of internships include:

Development and application of preclinical models for the evaluation of novel cancer therapies
Conservation Biogeography of insect-plant interactions: a multi-scalar geosystemic approach
Critical Transitions in Complex Socio-Ecological Systems: Integration of Satellite Observation, Artificial Intelligence, and Hybrid Dynamical Models
THRESHOLD: Thresholds, Gaps, and Alternatives for Living Well within Socio-Ecological Limits
Conservation Biogeography of insect-plant interactions: a multi-scalar geosystemic approach
Methods for Conducting a Thesis or Research in the Social Sciences

Firmware Engineer Co-op/Internship.Job Description::
Job Purpose

In keeping with the vision and mission of the Company, the Firmware Testing Co-Op/Internship role is responsible for the implementation and design of tests to help increase the robustness and speed of identifying firmware issues. Responsibilities will include, but are not limited to, implementing tests, debugging and documenting test failures, and investigating methods to improve test processes and features. Main interactions for this position will be within the firmware team but can extend to external teams.

Duties and Responsibilities

Firmware development
Design, implement, and maintain embedded firmware in C for microcontroller- or processor-based systems.
Develop new firmware features based on product requirements and engineering specifications.
Modify and optimize existing firmware for improved performance, memory usage, and power efficiency.
Debug firmware issues using hardware debugging tools such as JTAG/SWD debuggers, logic analyzers, and oscilloscopes.
Firmware Testing (C++)
Develop automated unit and integration tests in C++ to verify firmware functionality.
Execute regression tests to ensure new firmware changes do not introduce defects.
Investigate test failures and collaborate with firmware developers to resolve issues.
Improve test coverage by adding new automated test cases for firmware modules.
Maintain and enhance existing firmware test frameworks.
Firmware Engineer Co-op/Internship.Qualifications::
Qualifications

Currently enrolled in a Bachelor's or Master's degree program in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, or a related field.
Proficiency in C, C++ and Python for debugging and test automation.
Strong debugging skills, especially with Ethernet-connected devices.
Experience or coursework in network protocols (TCP/IP, UDP, etc.) is a plus.
Familiarity with embedded systems, firmware, and hardware debugging tools.
Self-motivated, eager to learn, and capable of working independently.
Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills.

FPGA Co-Op / Internship.Job Description::
Job Purpose

We are seeking a highly motivated and talented FPGA Engineer Co-op/Intern to join our team. In this role, you will be responsible for designing, implementing, and testing digital circuits using Field Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs). You will also be responsible for ensuring that these circuits meet the performance, power, and area requirements of our products.

Duties and Responsibilities

Design, implement, and test digital circuits using FPGAs
Collaborate with other members of the engineering team to ensure that FPGA designs meet product requirements
Utilize industry-standard FPGA design tools and methodologies to implement and test designs
Debug and troubleshoot FPGA designs as needed
Conduct performance analysis to ensure that FPGA designs meet performance, power, and area requirements
Document design processes and results, and participate in design reviews
FPGA Co-Op / Internship.Qualifications::
Qualifications

Currently enrolled in a Bachelor's or Master's degree program in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, or a related field
Strong understanding of digital logic design and experience with VHDL or Verilog programming
Familiarity with FPGA design tools and methodologies, such as Xilinx Vivado or Altera Quartus
Knowledge of high-speed digital design principles, signal integrity, and power management
Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Ability to work independently and in a team environment
Familiarity with embedded system design and programming
Experience with software development using languages such as C/C++ and Python
Familiarity with computer architecture and microprocessor design
Experience with hardware description languages (HDLs) and simulation tools, such as ModelSim or Xilinx Vivado
This is a full-time co-op/internship position. The working hours and duration of the position will be determined based on the candidate's availability and the needs of the company. The work will be hybrid at home and in office.

Are you an Engineering student who is looking for a hands-on opportunity this Fall in your field of study? Are you interested in real world experience that you will not get anywhere else?

We are seeking a highly motivated and innovative student for an internship position. As a Mechanical, Biomedical or Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering Intern, you will have the opportunity to work closely with experts at CIM-TAC on various projects aimed at pushing the boundaries of innovation in manufacturing technology and renewable energy systems.

Position Summary

The Centre for Innovation in Manufacturing Technology Access Centre (CIM-TAC) is a unique part of our Red Deer Polytechnic community. The CIM-TAC turns leading edge ideas into reality by collaborating with our clients on innovative manufacturing solutions. Experts work to design and develop new products and processes and connect with partners throughout the province. The CIM-TAC is where ideas come to reality and designs come to life. Some recent projects involved use of cutting-edge additive manufacturing technologies (3D printing) for developing medical and health care devices, advanced modeling & simulation for design optimization, quality assurance using 3D scanners and robotic automation in welding.

Students in CIM-TAC are passionate about innovation and excited to learn in a unique environment. They are comfortable with work that is ambiguous and ever-changing. They will offer ideas and suggestions to their leaders and are prepared for those ideas to become learning challenges.

Position Responsibilities

Assist in the design, analysis, and optimization of mechanical components and systems for manufacturing processes and renewable energy applications.
Support the development and testing of prototypes through hands-on work in the lab and workshop.
Conduct research and gather data to support engineering analysis and decision-making.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to solve engineering challenges and develop innovative solutions.
Actively participate in team meetings, brainstorming sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities.

Opportunities you will have by securing this position:

Hands-on experience working on real-world projects alongside industry experts.
Opportunity to contribute to research and innovation initiatives.
Mentorship and guidance from experienced professionals in the field.
Competitive compensation package.

Software Developer Co-op.Job Description::
Clio is the global leader in legal AI technology, empowering legal professionals and law firms of every size to work smarter, faster, and more securely.




Summary:

We are currently seeking a new Software Developer, Co-op to join our Engineering team out of hub offices (Toronto, Calgary, Burnaby). All hub-based Clions are expected to be in office minimum twice per week.

Applicants should be available for an 8-month co-op period from September 2026 to April 2026.

What your team does:

You will be working on a revolutionary SaaS product gaining experience with Ruby on Rails, AngularJS, React Native, MySQL, Redis, and ElasticSearch. You will be coached and mentored by some of the best developers in the business and working on some of the most challenging problems that a rapid growth venture-backed company can face.

What you'll work on:

Design, develop, and deploy high-quality software code as part of the Clio product suite.

Contribute actively to technical scoping and planning for upcoming development work.

Regularly communicate project progress and proactively address any impediments.

Engage in seamless collaboration with colleagues across the broader development organization.

Participate in thorough code reviews, both by submitting work for peer feedback and providing constructive input on others' contributions.

Drive the implementation of new features and deliver timely software solutions for customer-impacting issues.

Actively participate in various team-level and company-wide initiatives, contributing to strategic discussions that shape our engineering future.

Contribute to coordinating product support through a rotational assignment.

Commit to continuous professional development, actively seeking and providing feedback to foster a career as a highly skilled software developer.

.

What you will find here:

Compensation is one of the main components of Clio's Total Rewards Program. We have developed a series of programs and processes to ensure we are creating fair and competitive pay practices that form the foundation of our human and high-performing culture.

Some highlights of our Total Rewards program include:

Hybrid work environment, with expectation for local Clions (Vancouver, Calgary, Toronto, Dublin and Sydney) to be in office minimum twice per week.

4% vacation pay for hours worked.

The expected new hire base pay for this role is $67,000 CAD.

Diversity, Inclusion, Belonging and Equity (DIBE) & Accessibility

Our team shows up as their authentic selves, and are united by our mission. We are dedicated to diversity, equity and inclusion. We pride ourselves in building and fostering an environment where our teams feel included, valued, and enabled to do the best work of their careers, wherever they choose to log in from. We believe that different perspectives, skills, backgrounds, and experiences result in higher-performing teams and better innovation. We are committed to equal employment and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply.

Clio provides accessibility accommodations during the recruitment process. Should you require any accommodation, please let us know and we will work with you to meet your needs.

Learn more about our culture at clio.com/careers

We're a Human and High Performing AI company, meaning we use artificial intelligence to improve all of our operations. In recruitment, AI helps us streamline the process for greater efficiency. However, we've built our systems to ensure that a human always reviews AI-generated output, and we never make automated hiring decisions.

Disclaimer: We only communicate with candidates through official @clio.com email addresses.

Co-op Hardware Designer.Job Description::
WE BUILD SOLUTIONS THAT HELP SAVE LIVES

Zetron, a Codan company, is a trusted provider of communication products and solutions that help our customers protect, inform, and save lives. Whether they are conducting a search and rescue, containing a fire, maintaining safety, or providing security at a profile event, Zetron makes sure the customer can be heard over the mountain, throughout the city, or across the globe. We invite you to join our mission at our Canadian Head Office located near beautiful downtown Victoria in the newly restored Press Building with modern office space built for collaboration!

THE ROLE:

This is a minimum 4-month co-op based in Victoria, BC, with a strong preference for 8 months.

At Zetron, we hire co-op students not only because we want to invest in developers, but also because it is an investment in our business. Did you know that 40% of our Engineering staff are UVic alumni?

We are recruiting for a Co-op Hardware Designer who can join our Hardware team as we develop the next generation of Codan radios. We develop our radio systems entirely in-house, including the hardware, firmware, software, and user interface.

As a member of the Zetron Team, you won't be tasked with an isolated project. We will train you to be a contributing member of our hardware team and throughout the course of your co-op term, you will:

Work directly on new product development and help resolve manufacturing issues.
Hands on training and mentoring with members of the hardware team.
Use industry standard equipment (i.e., Spectrum analyzers, Network analyzers, Signal Analyzers, Oscilloscopes, ESD guns, Thermal camera, Signal Generators, Power Meters, Signal Generators, Thermal Chambers, Radio Test Sets, Soldering & Rework equipment, etc.) to validate designs or diagnose issues.
Learn Industry Communication standards (i.e., TIA-603, TIA-102) and design for test and manufacturability.
Co-op Hardware Designer.Qualifications::
ABOUT YOU:

We are looking for an ambitious co-op student, excited to take part in all aspects of the hardware development cycle who:

Has good RF and Analog circuit knowledge.
Is detailed orientated and loves to identify and resolve problems.
Uses strong communication and collaboration skills.
Is familiar with soldering and lab equipment.
Has proven organizational, time management and prioritizing skill.
Is eager to learn, grow, and be challenged.

This co-op position is offered based on an annualized salary range of $48,880 - $52,000 (equivalent to $23.50 - $25.00 per hour). Wages offered will be determined based on the successful candidate's assessed skills, experience and qualifications.

Please include resume, cover letter, and transcript without any links (plain text is fine) to any websites in your application package. Only successful applicants will be contacted for interviews. Thank you!

Computer Vision Engineering Intern.Job Description::
The Role
AIM is a deep-tech startup building advanced high-power laser systems to protect people from weaponised drones. We are seeking highly motivated Computer Vision Engineering Interns based in Vancouver, BC, Canada.


At AIM, you will be working with our Engineering and R&D team to create training data and develop machine learning models for use in tomorrow's defence technology.


The Commitment
We are looking for candidates who are available immediately on a full-time basis and are able to work effectively in a fast-paced, delivery-focused environment.


Compensation is $30 per hour.
Due to clearance and security requirements, this role is only open to Canadian Citizens with the ability to obtain and maintain the required security clearance.


What You'll do
Prepare machine-learning training and validation datasets using real-world image and video data
Develop software to simplify and automate manual steps of the training process
Analyse model performance using quantitative metrics (e.g. precision, recall, latency) and iterate to improve robustness and reliability
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (software, hardware, and R&D) to integrate with our laser platform
Document experiments, methodologies, and results clearly to support continuous improvement and knowledge sharing
Ensure work is delivered to a high standard, meets timelines, and results in production-ready software deliverables
Computer Vision Engineering Intern.Qualifications::
What You'll bring
At least 2 years of computer science, computer engineering, or mechatronics engineering education (i.e. 3rd year students and above)
You've taken courses in, or have worked with, neural networks and machine learning
Experience (educational or professional) with at least one of the following programming languages: C, C++, Python, or Go
Strong time management skills, with the ability to prioritise tasks, manage competing deadlines, and adapt to changing requirements
Ability to work both independently and collaboratively within a multidisciplinary team
How You'll Benefit
Competitive remuneration
Exciting projects and industry leading innovations.
Professional learning and development opportunities.
Opportunity to innovate and work in a fast-paced team.
Challenges at the edge of what's possible.
A commitment to ensuring diversity, inclusion, and equality is embedded throughout our organisation for the benefit of our customers and our team. We strive for a positive and engaging workplace where mental health and wellbeing are supported. We welcome applicants from all diverse backgrounds.


If you are looking for a unique and exciting opportunity, we look forward to your application.

Firmware Engineer Intern.Job Description::
.Astera Labs (NASDAQ: ALAB) provides rack-scale AI infrastructure through purpose-built connectivity solutions. By collaborating with hyperscalers and ecosystem partners, Astera Labs enables organizations to unlock the full potential of modern AI. Astera Labs' Intelligent Connectivity Platform integrates CXL®, Ethernet, NVLink, PCIe®, and UALink™ semiconductor-based technologies with the company's COSMOS software suite to unify diverse components into cohesive, flexible systems that deliver end-to-end scale-up, and scale-out connectivity. The company's custom connectivity solutions business complements its standards-based portfolio, enabling customers to deploy tailored architectures to meet their unique infrastructure requirements. Discover more at www.asteralabs.com.

Job Description:

The mission of this role is to architect and develop firmware and microcontroller subsystems for Astera Labs' SoC and systems products. Firmware is responsible for implementing the major differentiating features of Astera Labs' products. As such, firmware is considered equally important to the hardware, and the firmware team is often customer-facing accordingly to ensure the needs of the customer are fully comprehended.
Firmware Engineer Intern.Qualifications::
Basic qualifications:

Strong academic and technical background in electrical engineering. At a minimum, a Bachelor's in EE or Computer Science is required.
Completed one or more Co-op terms of firmware or embedded software development or up to 3 years of industry experience.
Completed one or more courses in Real Time Operating Systems (RTOS) or detailed use of an RTOS during project work or prior role.
Professional attitude with the ability to formulate theories and well thought out questions as part of helping debug/develop complex systems.
Authorized to work in Canada and can start immediately.

Required experience:

Proficiency in C (preferred) or C++, including development of firmware.
Proficient in Python for scripting.
Ability to design, implement, and write unit-level tests.
Experience with developer workflows, SCM (preferably git), code reviews, CI.
Experience developing firmware to execute in on-chip microcontrollers.
Experience debugging firmware/hardware issues using JTAG, trace logs, or other lab tools.

Preferred experience:

Knowledge of tools like PyTest or GoogleTest is an asset.
Knowledge of software build environments, CI/CD, gcc/make is an asset
Experience with lab hardware/firmware debug is an asset.
Experience developing firmware for PCIe/CXL/Ethernet is an asset.

Electronics Design Co-op (Fall 2026).Job Description::
At Blue Robotics, we enable the field of marine robotics with affordable components and systems. What does this really mean? We design cool tech for use underwater that allows tons of people and organizations to pursue their audacious dreams of exploring and learning about our oceans and waterways.

We have a small and vibrant design team in Victoria, BC with a collaborative spirit that is both humble and bold. We work together to navigate all design steps from the whiteboard to fully tested, volume-manufactured products.

We are looking for a fledgling electronics designer - someone with some PCB experience and a hunger to crank this up a few notches. If you:

have an innate desire to create new things,
noodle intently over your projects, and
yearn to expand your technical depth and understanding,

then you'll likely be a great fit!

Will it all be whipped cream and roses? Probably not. But as a team we'll chip away at this awesome, fun and sometimes infuriating dream that we've decided deserves to become a reality. And don't worry - you won't get stuck on a manufacturing line - you'll design real products and you'll be given the same responsibility as any of our full-time engineers.

As an Analyst (Intern) in the Credit Risk Analytics team at National Bank, you will develop valuable risk management skills by working on credit risk modeling and risk reporting in areas like IFRS-9, stress testing, and capital. You will collaborate closely with the team and perform various risk analysis tasks to support the Bank's risk function. With your programming, data analysis, problem-solving, and communication skills, you will gain hands-on experience and a deeper understanding of risk management. This is a full-time internship.

Your role:

Contribute to risk analytics and reporting, including the preparation of analysis results and presentations for senior management.
Participate in the development, implementation and back testing of credit risk models, including Probability of Default (PD), Loss Given Default (LGD), Exposure at Default (EAD) and other models.
Support the monthly and quarterly risk production activities, quantify risk and capital, and identify key risk drivers.
Create and maintain documents for models and risk processes following the internal and regulatory requirements.
Complete risk-relevant tasks assigned by the supervisor and respond to questions and ad-hoc requests from the senior management in a precise and efficient manner.

Your team

As part of the Credit Risk Analytics sector, you'll be on a team of 25 of colleagues. Our team stands out for expertise in model development, model implementation and risk reporting in IFRS-9, stress testing and capital areas. As some processes are part of the Bank's month-end or quarter-end reporting to the public or to the regulators, we may work under tight deadline and pressure sometimes. We offer a wide range of ongoing learning opportunities for your development, including coaching, hands-on learning, and through the collaborating with colleagues who have varied expertise and profiles such as risk programming, in-depth data analytics, model development, and presentation to senior management.




Your benefits

In addition to competitive compensation, you'll be eligible for a wide range of flexible benefits to help promote your wellbeing and that of your family such as:

* Health and wellness program, including many options* Group insurance* Generous pension plan* Employee and Family Assistance Program* Preferential banking services* Opportunities to get involved in community initiatives* Telemedicine service* Virtual sleep clinic

We have an offer that keeps up with trends as well as your needs and those of your family.

Our dynamic work environments and cutting-edge collaboration tools foster a positive employee experience. We actively listen to employees' ideas. Whether through our surveys or programs, regular feedback and ongoing communication is encouraged.

Making a bold move in a people-first environment

We're a bank on a human scale that stands out for its courage, entrepreneurial culture, and passion for people.Our mission is to have a positive impact on people's lives. Our core values of partnership, agility, and empowerment inspire us, and inclusion is central to our commitments. We aim, wherever possible, to provide a barrier-free and accessible environment to all employees.

We strive to provide accessibility measures throughout the recruitment process within the limits of our available resources. If you require accommodations, feel free to let us know during our initial conversations. We welcome all candidates! What can you bring to our team

Software Engineering co-op.Job Description::
About Moment Energy

Independent energy is here. Help us deploy it.

Energy should be free. Free from aging grids, fragile supply chains, and unnecessary extraction. At Moment Energy, we're building a world where clean power is abundant, affordable, and uninterrupted for everyone.

The world doesn't need another promise about energy; it needs proof that a freer future is possible. Moment Energy is the pragmatic liberator of power, the only company certified to turn the largest untapped energy supply into independent energy.

Now is the moment. While the industry scraps millions of batteries with decades of life left, we put them to work by repurposing large-format batteries into advanced battery energy storage systems that deliver real-world impact.

We were told second-life batteries would never be safe enough or scalable enough. We didn't argue, we engineered the solution. Today, Moment Energy delivers 5x the energy density and holds the certifications required to deploy energy independence at scale.

Now is the moment. Join us.

The Opportunity

We are seeking a Software Engineering Co-op to join our Engineering team. In this role, you will support the development of the cloud platform that powers our battery management systems and energy storage products.

You will work on both front-end and back-end development while collaborating with engineers across hardware and software teams. This is a great opportunity for a student who wants to gain hands-on experience building scalable software systems for real-world energy infrastructure.

Responsibilities and Duties

Develop front-end web interfaces for the battery management system (BMS) cloud portal
Contribute to backend API development and system architecture
Design and implement database schemas to support platform functionality
Help integrate energy storage hardware with cloud services and IoT infrastructure
Develop unit tests and integration tests to ensure system reliability and stability
Maintain high standards of code quality, documentation, and reusability
Support development of the IoT platform for remote battery monitoring and control
Assist with software testing, debugging, and performance improvements
Collaborate with engineers across hardware and software teams to support product development

The Student we're looking for:

Currently pursuing a Bachelor's degree or higher in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related discipline
Strong preference for students with Design Team or previous co-op experience
Experience with modern web development frameworks and tools
Familiarity with backend development and API design
Basic understanding of databases and data modeling
Interest in cloud platforms, IoT systems, and connected hardware
Experience with version control systems such as Git
Strong problem-solving and analytical skills
Ability to work both independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced engineering environment

The Experience You'll Gain:

Hands-on experience developing software for real-world energy infrastructure
Exposure to cloud platforms, IoT systems, and hardware-software integration
Mentorship from experienced software and systems engineers
The opportunity to contribute to technology supporting the clean energy transition

Moment Energy Co-op Benefits Highlights

Paid Time Off: Co-op's receive three paid days off per term

Vibrant Culture & Perks: Open and collaborative office culture, frequent company socials and wellness programs (weekly yoga and fitness center), and free EV charging on-site.

Why join us?

Today, we're delivering 5x the energy density and have all the certifications needed to ship energy independence to the world.
Be part of a mission-driven company focused on creating sustainable energy solutions and making a positive impact on the world.
Collaborate closely with passionate co-founders and a team committed to innovation and excellence.
Contribute to building and scaling a company from the ground up, where your work will have a direct and meaningful impact.
Thrive in a workplace that values collaboration, wellness, and celebrating successes, with regular company socials, fitness sessions, and more.

We're a fast-paced and dynamic team.

Every day you'll be taking on a new challenge and learning something new. You'll take on a wide breadth of tasks and contribute to disrupting the battery industry. You'll be part of a tight-knit team that is working together to save the planet! Fun, health, and wellness are incorporated into our daily schedule and we encourage all our team members to bring innovative ideas to the table to create an interactive workplace.
